content_of_post_to_comment:
It's wild how they call it chill when it's just filled with rude assholes, cunt's touchy winers and clingy fuck's that downvote the fuck out of you and play favorites and people who are just behaving like outcast

Example: I made a post warning people about some YT channel making fetish and NSFW art and like to report it. But instead, I was downvoted and people said I should be sued and to fuck off.

Then why in the fuck do you say yes to report others and not the one I talked about you fucking moron?

Also fuck everyone in that sub, all of them are so fucking toxic and rude, always go off topic and even just attack you, and fuck the mods as well, I can tell most of them are frustrated dweebs that got bullied in school or obese fucks that want to feel the power on the internet. It's all hopeless at this point. Like whenever I post anything like a UI change or some YouTube channel that's violating YT's policies and YouTube isn't doing anything about it, I get attacked by people in that sub. And worst of all the mods on that sub don't do anything about it like YouTube does at all.

If you want to achieve real no surf, r/youtube needs to go because it's just a rabbit whole of toxicity. Maybe share your experience about the sub like I did?
comment: I get why that reaction would be frustrating, especially when you reported something you believed broke YouTube's rules and the response was just hostility. At the same time, big communities often become defensive when posts sound like calls to action against creators or users. It might be worth looking at how the concern was framed too, because even a valid point can get buried if people feel attacked.
